ERROR.GEOLOCATION_NOT_SUPPORTED: Geolocation is not supported by your browser
ERROR.INVALID_LATITUDE_FORMAT: Latitude must be a numeric value
ERROR.INVALID_LONGITUDE_FORMAT: Longitude must be a numeric value
ERROR.USERNAME_TOO_SHORT: Username must be at least 3 characters long.
ERROR.PASSWORD_TOO_SHORT: New password must be at least 8 characters long.

LABEL.OBJECT_TYPES_HELP: Filter by object classification tags from ANTARES
LABEL.OBSERVATION_NOTES_PLACEHOLDER: What did you see? How were the conditions?
LABEL.RA_TOOLTIP: Right Ascension — celestial longitude, in degrees (0–360)
LABEL.DEC_TOOLTIP: Declination — celestial latitude, in degrees (−90 to +90)
LABEL.RARE_CLASSIFICATION_TAGS_PLACEHOLDER: Type a tag or choose from the list
LABEL.PRESET_NAME_HELP: Give this combination a memorable name (e.g. "Supernovae only")
LABEL.NO_PRESETS_HINT: Adjust the filters below, then click Save Preset to memorize this combination and switch back in one click.
LABEL.FILTER_PRESETS_HELP: Apply a saved set of filters in one click, or save your current settings as a new preset.
LABEL.VISIBILITY_HELP: Only show objects visible during this window. Supports overnight ranges (e.g. 11:00 PM – 3:00 AM).

MESSAGE.ABOUT_PARA_1: Sky2nite helps amateur astronomers discover what celestial objects are visible from their location using real-time data from the ANTARES astronomical alert broker.
MESSAGE.ABOUT_PARA_2: ANTARES (Arizona-NOIRLab Temporal Analysis and Response to Events System) processes real-time alerts from the Vera C. Rubin Observatory's Legacy Survey of Space and Time (LSST) and other surveys, identifying transient astronomical phenomena.
MESSAGE.ABOUT_PARA_3: Most amateur telescopes can observe objects up to magnitude 12-14 in good viewing conditions. Darker skies and larger apertures allow viewing of fainter objects.
MESSAGE.APP_DESCRIPTION: Discover astronomical objects visible tonight from your location using real-time data from the ANTARES broker and the Vera C. Rubin Observatory.
MESSAGE.WRITTEN_BY: Code written with love (and a touch of AI) by Devin Major
MESSAGE.LOCATION_SUBTITLE: Enter your location to see which objects are visible tonight
MESSAGE.ALTITUDE_HELP: Objects must reach this altitude above the horizon to appear in results. Higher values mean less atmospheric interference.
MESSAGE.MAGNITUDE_HELP: Brighter objects have lower magnitude values. Most consumer telescopes can see objects up to magnitude 12-14 in good conditions.
MESSAGE.MIN_ALERTS_HELP: Type a minimum ANTARES alert count. Higher values surface repeat detections and ongoing transients; the default is 5.
MESSAGE.LOCUS_ID_SEARCH_HELP: Search by exact Locus ID (e.g. ANT2021y65ce). Partial name matching is limited to the brightest objects.
MESSAGE.ALERT_ACTIVITY_TOOLTIP: Higher alert counts are a rough proxy for multi-night consistency and activity.
MESSAGE.CLASSIFICATION_RARITY_HINT: Rare or high-interest classifications are highlighted in the table.
MESSAGE.ALERT_ACTIVITY_CURVE_TOOLTIP: This sparkline shows a recent alert timeline for the locus, binned from the oldest sampled alert to the newest. Taller bars mean more alerts in that part of the sample.
MESSAGE.NAME_FILTER_NEEDS_LOCATION: Type a Locus ID above and click Search Sky Objects to search by name, or enter a location first to see all visible objects.
MESSAGE.NO_OBJECTS_DESCRIPTION: No astronomical objects matching your criteria are visible tonight from your location. Try adjusting the magnitude threshold or removing object type filters.
MESSAGE.PAGINATION_CONTEXT: Page {{page}} — up to {{pageSize}} visible objects fetched per page
MESSAGE.SEARCHING_SUBTITLE: Querying ANTARES database and calculating visibility
MESSAGE.SETTINGS_DESCRIPTION: Manage app settings and login credentials.
MESSAGE.APPEARANCE_DESCRIPTION: Customize the visual experience of the app.
MESSAGE.PARTICLE_EFFECTS_DESCRIPTION: Animated star field with collision effects. Disable to reduce GPU usage.
MESSAGE.RARE_CLASSIFICATION_DESCRIPTION: Choose which classifications should be highlighted as rare or high-interest, and pick the color palette for their badges.
MESSAGE.RARE_CLASSIFICATION_TAGS_HELP: Select from the ANTARES object types list or type your own tag.
MESSAGE.MY_OBSERVATIONS_DESCRIPTION: Celestial objects you've saved across all your viewing sessions.
MESSAGE.NO_SAVED_OBSERVATIONS: You haven't saved any observations yet. Click the bookmark icon on any object to save it here.
MESSAGE.PRESET_NAME_REQUIRED: Please enter a name for this preset.
MESSAGE.PRESET_UPDATE_OVERWRITE: A preset named "{{name}}" already exists. Update it with the current filters?
